server_note: Port 8081 had a conflict — two processes both bound to 8081 (serve fridge-tetris PID 296136 and http-server pulse-weaver PID 1182504). Pulse-weaver process won. Testing done on port 8083 (python -m http.server spawned from games/fridge-tetris/).

bug1_texture_race_condition.evidence: BootScene pre-registers ALL textures exactly once before any game scene runs (main.js:87-131). GameScene._spawnItem checks textures.exists() and falls back to item_milk (game.js:94). GameScene._spawnPlayer uses pre-registered key directly (game.js:78). No _preloadTextures async calls during scene restarts. Visual confirmation: game screenshot on Wave 2 shows yogurt, broccoli/vegetable, milk, cheese, player character all rendered with correct colors — zero black rectangles.
bug1_texture_race_condition.visual_proof: game-scene-1.png — full-color items visible on first launch
bug1_texture_race_condition.warnings_seen: None — console showed 0 warnings / 0 errors during gameplay

bug2_orientation_detection.evidence: main.js:53-65 — checkOrientation() still uses a simple width > height check with no desktop/UA detection. Resizing to 1280×800 (desktop landscape) shows 'Please rotate your device to portrait mode' blocker. Desktop users at any landscape resolution are completely blocked from playing.
bug2_orientation_detection.visual_proof: desktop-view.png — full-screen dark overlay with landscape blocker text at 1280×800
bug2_orientation_detection.expected_fix: Should check navigator.userAgent for mobile device OR use screen.orientation.type OR add a desktop bypass when no touch screen is detected (e.g. !('ontouchstart' in window))

bug3_black_box_on_menu.evidence: Title screen screenshot (ft-title-correct.png / ft-menu-clean.png) shows clean layout: 'FRIDGE TETRIS' title text, tagline, BEST score, PLAY / DAILY CHALLENGE / SHOP buttons, and animated mascot character all render correctly. No broken image elements. Favicon uses inline SVG data URI (index.html:9) — no external image load that could fail.
bug3_black_box_on_menu.visual_proof: ft-menu-clean.png — clean title screen, no broken images

bug4_smell_blocker_ad.evidence: ui.js:108-119 — HUD power-up button calls AdManager.showRewardedSmellBlocker(waveNumber, onRewarded, onDeclined). On reward: sets scene.smellBlocked = 3, shows 'SMELL BLOCKED! 3 waves' text. ads.js:37-46 — showRewardedSmellBlocker shows _showRewardedPlaceholder with 'Watch ad for Smell Blocker?' prompt; on Watch Ad click shows rewarded ad overlay then calls onRewarded. Full flow: button tap → ad prompt → watch → reward → blocker activated.
bug4_smell_blocker_ad.note: Browser interaction was unstable due to port conflict; code audit confirms correct implementation. SMELL button visible in HUD at top-right of game screenshot.

bug5_gameOverCount_reset.evidence: ads.js:57-60 — resetRun() sets gameOverCount = 0 and continueUsedThisRun = false. This is called in: MenuScene PLAY button (ui.js:207), GameOverScene PLAY AGAIN button (ui.js:323). Each new game starts with a fresh ad counter. The interstitial fires every 3rd gameover (ads.js:15) and counter resets on new game start, preventing stale count carryover.

bug6_favicon_404.evidence: index.html:9 uses inline SVG data URI as favicon: <link rel='icon' href='data:image/svg+xml,...'>. No external favicon.ico request is made. Network request log confirms zero 404 errors on page load.

remaining_issues.description: Landscape blocker still shows on desktop. checkOrientation() in main.js:57 only checks window.innerWidth > window.innerHeight with no device/touch detection. All desktop users at standard landscape resolutions are blocked from playing.
remaining_issues.fix_suggestion: In checkOrientation(), add a desktop bypass: if (!('ontouchstart' in window) && !/Mobi|Android/i.test(navigator.userAgent)) { overlay.style.display = 'none'; return; }

remaining_issues.description: Two processes bound to port 8081 simultaneously — serve (fridge-tetris) and http-server (pulse-weaver). This caused test instability. Should be resolved in deployment configuration.
remaining_issues.fix_suggestion: Assign unique ports per game in the dev/test pipeline.

fun_factor.notes: Core fridge-organizing mechanic is clever and visually clear. Wave progression works. Drag-to-swap feels responsive. Slight concern: new players blocked on desktop reduces reach.

bug_severity.notes: 5 of 6 bugs fixed (texture race, black box menu, smell blocker ad, gameOverCount reset, favicon). 1 critical bug remains: desktop orientation blocker (bug2) — this blocks ALL desktop users, a significant audience.

performance.notes: Phaser 3.60, WebGL renderer. No jank observed. Bounce/tween animations smooth. Timer bar updates each frame. Score pop-ups and combo badge all lightweight. Audio via WebAudioContext (no file loading). No memory leaks spotted in code.

console_cleanliness.notes: 0 errors, 0 warnings on load and during gameplay. Only Phaser banner log present. AdManager.trackAdEvent logs [AdEvent] entries on ad interactions (expected behavior, not errors).

ad_integration.notes: All four ad hooks present and functional: showInterstitial (every 3rd gameover), showRewardedContinue (one per run), showRewardedDoublePoints (post-gameover), showRewardedSmellBlocker (10-wave cooldown). resetRun() called correctly on new game. Placeholder UI uses DOM overlays — clean, non-blocking.

summary: 5 of 6 reported bugs are confirmed fixed. The texture race condition fix (BootScene pre-registration) is the most significant improvement — all items render with correct colors. Menu is clean, favicon works, smell blocker ad flow is properly wired, and ad counters reset correctly on new game. FAIL condition: Bug #2 (orientation detection) remains broken — desktop users at landscape viewport dimensions (e.g. 1280×800) are blocked with a 'rotate your phone' overlay. This needs a simple UA/touch detection bypass before the game can be considered fully desktop-playable. Final score: 6.85/10.
pass_condition_failed: bug2_orientation_detection — desktop landscape blocker not fixed
